intro: Symplococarpon purpusii, commonly known as Mexican copal, is a plant species belonging to the family Ebenaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Mexican copal include phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Mexican copal include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and sore throats.
